medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Presented with 1 month history of episodic sharp lower abdominal pain relieved by Demerol. He started to have suprapubic and LLQ abdominal pain about a month ago. While he was in another location, his pain worsened, and he was hospitalized there. He describes the pain as crampy and sharp, not associated with eating. He has constipation, bowel incontinence, occasional night sweats, and a fever of 100.5 at home. He lost 12 lb over the past month.
medical_history.past_medical_history:
- Hereditary Telangectasia with pulmonary arterial venous malformations
- Pulmonary shunts s/p two previous closure procedures
- Nosebleeds
- Systemic emboli
- TIA
- Iron deficiency anemia
medical_history.family_history: Mother had well-controlled HTN and DMII. No family history of hereditary telangectasia or epistaxis.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital signs: T: 100.4, BP: 137/85, HR: 112, RR: 18, O2 sat: 97% on room air. General: NAD. HEENT: sclera anicteric, MM slightly dry. Cardiac: tachycardia, regular rhythm. Normal S1, S2.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: AOx3, CNII-XII intact. Abdomen: normoactive BS, tender to deep palpation at suprapubic and LLQ. Rectal exam: purply tender lesions outside the anus, purply soft AVM-like lesions protruding out of the anus.
